title: Task outcome: task-20260706-161246-Completion-Reviewer-09-read-
summary: Completion Reviewer 09 completed a read-only Observatory/UI/graph review. Conclusion: agree with the proposed direction only if completion requires live, non-stale Observatory evidence: fresh graph health over the real accepted corpus, live graph/status endpoints tied to real wiki/operations indexes, visible memory-use audits, active-task hygiene, explicit blocked/pending/verifier/main states, and graph design that is operational rather than decorative. Key risk found: persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy while an in-memory rebuild reports degraded score 0 with 154 accepted records lacking durable source mapping.
claim: Task outcome for DinoBrain: Completion Reviewer 09 completed a read-only Observatory/UI/graph review. Conclusion: agree with the proposed direction only if completion requires live, non-stale Observatory evidence: fresh graph health over the real accepted corpus, live graph/status endpoints tied to real wiki/operations indexes, visible memory-use audits, active-task hygiene, explicit blocked/pending/verifier/main states, and graph design that is operational rather than decorative. Key risk found: persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy while an in-memory rebuild reports degraded score 0 with 154 accepted records la
decisions: Observatory completion must be defined by visible operational truth, not only a passing graph UI fixture.
Graph-health freshness must be a gate; the current persisted graph-health file is stale relative to the wiki index and accepted corpus.
The live graph should prioritize semantic clusters, search, hover detail, and state visibility, and avoid dinosaur/fossil/decorative visual metaphors as completion criteria.
Active tasks must distinguish actually-running work from stale started records and show blocked/pending/verifier/main lanes explicitly.
Memory-use visibility must show provided, declared-used, observed-used, missing expected, hallucinated references, trust score, and graph-health snapshot, not just an audit count.
next_steps: Regenerate graph health after wiki/operations index updates and fail readiness when graph-health is older than the index or inconsistent with accepted record count.
Add/verify an Observatory real-data health card that compares persisted graph-health to a fresh sampled health computation and flags stale false-healthy states.
Add task-state hygiene for stale started tasks and explicit lanes for main, verifier/reviewer, pending, blocked, completed/partial.
Backfill durable source/provenance records or explicitly classify internal trace-only behavior memories so graph health can distinguish behavior memory lineage from source-truth lineage.
Expand Observatory verification from synthetic fixture to real-data endpoint assertions covering active tasks, memory audit count, graph-health freshness, and no decorative graph terminology.
